objective: Build a finite executable version of both the quantum-Darwinism redundancy plateau and a distributed-systems replication factor and test whether the correspondence is more than vocabulary.
qd_model: system qubit |+>, N=8 environment qubits each an imperfect copy via controlled Ry(theta). Exact partial information I(S:F); redundancy R_delta = N/f_delta, f_delta = smallest fragment reaching (1-delta)H(S).

quantitative_test.reading: R_delta is NOT delta-invariant, so it is not yet a well-defined replication factor.

verdict_text: CONFIRMED correspondence, NOT yet a discovery. The redundancy plateau (many disjoint fragments each independently sufficient) is structurally the eventual-consistency replication property (any read quorum reconstructs the value; record survives losing all but one quorum) -- more than vocabulary. But the quantitative map R_delta = replication factor is unbuilt: R_delta depends on delta, and the DS replication factor depends on the failure model and quorum-intersection rule; no shared dimensionless invariant computes the same number on both sides.
wake_condition: find a dimensionless invariant (candidate: information deficit 1 - f_delta/N vs a quorum-intersection redundancy) that both sides compute identically; if it exists, the bridge converts from analogy to discovery.
